name: Jordan Rivera
title: Staff Software Engineer
summary: I build product-facing systems at the intersection of developer experience and user experience — TypeScript and React on the frontend, Node.js and Go on the backend, and increasingly LLM-powered features throughout. I've shipped production AI integrations, owned design systems in code, and led small engineering teams through zero-to-one builds. I care about code that ships fast, scales cleanly, and delights the people using it.

bullets: Tech lead for the product's core workflow engine — a React/TypeScript frontend and Node.js backend serving 12,000 B2B users across 400 enterprise accounts.
Designed and shipped an LLM-powered document analysis feature that reduced manual data extraction by 70%; became the product's top sales differentiator within two quarters.
Built and maintained the internal component library (80+ components, Radix primitives, Tailwind CSS), cutting new feature UI development time by 35%.
Led a 3-engineer squad to replatform a legacy PHP monolith onto a Next.js App Router + PostgreSQL + tRPC stack; shipped in 14 weeks with zero downtime.
Established AI-assisted development standards across the engineering org: Claude Code + Cursor workflows that measurably improved individual output velocity.
Built the core data visualization layer for a fintech analytics dashboard used by 2,000+ portfolio managers; shipped complex charting in React with real-time WebSocket updates.
Architected a Node.js microservices layer that decoupled 5 legacy integrations, reducing average API latency from 800ms to 95ms.
Shipped a zero-to-one mobile web experience in React that drove a 28% increase in monthly active users within the first 60 days.
Mentored 4 junior engineers through code review, pair programming, and architecture conversations; 3 were promoted to mid-level within 18 months.
Full-stack development on React/Rails SaaS products for media and publishing clients.
Led migration of 3 client products from jQuery/PHP to modern React frontends.
Built an internal CMS tooling layer used across 12 active client projects; reduced content management ops time by 40%.

writing_rules: Active voice. Concrete metrics over adjectives.
No closing flourish or restatement sentences.
